title: Robust Dense Greek Red Snapper Revithia
description: A kid-friendly Greek dish featuring red snapper, with a tangy and dense texture, finished to an al-dente perfection. This Jain-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional revithia.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chickpeas to the bowl and toss to coat with the dressing.
Place the red snapper fillets in a separate bowl and brush with the remaining dressing.
In a large baking dish, create a layer of chickpeas, followed by a layer of red snapper fillets.
Repeat the layers until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of chickpeas on top.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
